title: Community
summary: Connect with the cross-government developer community through Slack, events, blogs, and open source.

items.title: Developer Slack
items.description: Join the Ministry of Justice Digital & Technology Slack workspace and the main developer support channels.

sections.heading: Key channels
sections.body: Use the workspace to ask questions, discover what other teams are building, and find the right platform owners more quickly.

sections.heading: When to use it
bullets: To ask for early delivery advice
To find service owners and specialist communities
To share useful patterns and learn from other teams

items.title: Open Source
items.description: Browse Ministry of Justice open source projects and contribute in the open where appropriate.

sections.heading: What you will find
sections.body: The GitHub organisation includes platform tooling, reusable libraries, service code, and examples of working in the open.

sections.heading: How to use it
bullets: Look for existing patterns before building something new
Open issues or pull requests where contribution is appropriate
Use repository READMEs to understand ownership and setup

items.title: Engineering Blog
items.description: Read about what teams are building, technical decisions, and lessons learned across the portal and related work.

sections.heading: Typical content
bullets: Examples of reusable patterns across teams

items.title: Events and Meetups
items.description: Regular tech talks, show-and-tells, and cross-government meetups for sharing practice.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: Events help teams compare approaches, reduce duplicate effort, and build stronger cross-team relationships.

sections.heading: Attendance
bullets: Open to engineering, design, delivery, and platform teams
Join in person or remotely where available
Share talk ideas and topics with Developer Experience

links.label: GDS API technical and data standards

contribution.title: Contributing to the Developer Portal
contribution.summary: This portal is open source and accepts contributions. You can:
actions: Add your team's documentation by creating a portal.yaml in your repo
Suggest new products or guidelines via pull request
Report issues or request features on GitHub
